prompt:
Transform [SUBJECT IMAGE] into a candid action portrait captured at the exact moment a soccer ball collides with the side of their face. Preserve the subject’s identity and clothing unless changed intentionally. Expression should look naturally interrupted: eyes squeezed shut or blinking, mouth slightly open, cheeks compressed from impact, hair moving from motion.
Composition: close-up portrait framing from chest upward. Place the soccer ball extremely close to the face (mid-impact), slightly deforming expression through timing and perspective rather than cartoon effects.
Background: bright outdoor sky with soft clouds and distant landscape, shallow depth of field.
Styling: documentary sports photography mixed with accidental yearbook energy, candid and imperfect. Natural daylight, slight motion blur, crisp skin detail, realistic physics, subtle humor.
Wardrobe should remain casual and everyday. Keep an authentic, unstaged feeling—as if captured one frame before and after impact.
Color grading: soft daylight tones, mild contrast, natural highlights, slightly nostalgic digital-camera look.
